task_completion.description: Standardize dates, determine success, and calculate rooting duration
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for new columns added to the original data containing standardized dates, a clear success/failure indicator (e.g., TRUE/FALSE, 1/0, 'Success' derived from messy outcomes like 'roots!' or 'rotted'), and a calculated duration in days.

task_completion.description: Calculate success rates by propagation method
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a summary table listing at least 3 propagation methods (e.g., water, soil, perlite, LECA, sphagnum) with their calculated success rates (percentages or fractions).

task_completion.description: Calculate success rates by plant type
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a summary table listing at least 5 plant types (e.g., Pothos, Monstera, String of Pearls, Philodendron, Snake Plant) with their calculated success rates.

task_completion.description: Identify fastest-rooting combinations by calculating average rooting times
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a column or table section showing the average number of days to root for different methods or plant types.

task_completion.description: Apply conditional formatting to create actionable insights
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for colored cells (e.g., green/red backgrounds, color scales, or data bars) in the summary tables highlighting the highest/lowest success rates or fastest/slowest rooting times.

task_completion.description: Save the final report as propagation_analysis.xlsx
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the application title bar or a save dialog showing the filename 'propagation_analysis.xlsx' saved in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used a spreadsheet application to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a GUI spreadsheet application like LibreOffice Calc being used, rather than a terminal script or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used formulas for calculations rather than hardcoding results
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the formula bar in the spreadsheet to ensure actual formulas (e.g., IF, DAYS, COUNTIF, AVERAGEIFS) are being used for data cleaning and aggregation.

integrity.description: The agent processed the actual provided dataset
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the data visible in the spreadsheet matches the synthetic houseplant propagation dataset (e.g., plant types like Pothos and Monstera, mixed date formats, informal outcomes).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 40 records with 7 plant types (Pothos, Monstera, String of Pearls, Philodendron, Snake Plant, ZZ Plant, Spider Plant) and 5 methods (water, soil, perlite, LECA, sphagnum). Outcomes are messy text (e.g., 'roots!', 'rotted', 'success'). Overall success rate is 72.5% (29/40). Expected success rates by method: Water 75%, Soil 37.5%, Perlite 37.5%, LECA 75%, Sphagnum 67%. Expected by plant: Pothos 100%, Monstera 50%, String of Pearls 25%, Philodendron 67%, Snake Plant 25%, Spider Plant 75%, ZZ Plant 20%. The agent must calculate these rates, determine rooting duration, find average rooting times, apply conditional formatting, and save as propagation_analysis.xlsx.
